checks.a_analysis: The output JSON accurately reflects the text content found in the source PDF across all six slides. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or instructions were found. The added section titles (e.g., 'Slide 1: MyNCID User Guide for MFA') are within the scope of acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.

The output contains the URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' multiple times, which matches the source document exactly. There are no other phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs in the source to verify, and no fabricated contact information was introduced.

The output document contains six sections corresponding to the six pages (slides) in the source PDF. All methods of enrollment (Email, Text, Phone Call, Authenticator), modification instructions, default method change instructions, and deletion instructions are present.
The source document contains no tables; the information is presented in lists and steps, which are fully captured in the output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All steps and descriptive text within each section are present in the output. This includes specific parenthetical notes (e.g., in Slide 6, step 2) and the 'Note' box on Slide 5. No significant content omissions were detected.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output follows the source precisely. Minor punctuation and whitespace variations are present but do not alter the meaning or instructions provided in the guide.

checks.a_analysis: The document title 'MyNCID User Guide for MFA' and various section headings (e.g., 'How to enroll for MFA:') appear repeatedly throughout the source PDF as headers or recurring titles on each page. The output faithfully reproduces these without adding unintended duplicates. Text blocks such as the numbered instructions are not repeated incorrectly.

The heading hierarchy in the output is flat and incorrect. The document title ('heading-1'), section headings ('heading-2', 'heading-8', etc.), and sub-headings (e.g., 'heading-3', 'heading-4') are all assigned level 3 (H3). This skips levels 1 (H1) and 2 (H2), failing to provide a logical structure for screen reader navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF has a clear hierarchical structure: Page titles (e.g., 'MyNCID User Guide for MFA'), major sections (e.g., 'How to enroll for MFA:'), and sub-sections (e.g., 'Email', 'Text Message').
checks.b_evidence_output: "id": "heading-1", "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "MyNCID User Guide for MFA" ... "id": "heading-2", "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "How to enroll for MFA:"

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; it utilizes columns and lists. Therefore, there is no table structure to evaluate.

The reading order in the output correctly reflects the logical flow of the multi-column layout in the source PDF. It processes columns from left-to-right on a per-page basis (e.g., Email, then Text Message, then Phone Call on Page 1), ensuring the instructions are sequential and logical.

On pages 3 and 5, list items containing inline icons are incorrectly converted. The text appearing after the icon is split into a separate paragraph element rather than being part of the list item. For example, on Page 3, 'In your profile page, click on [icon]' is the list item, but 'next to your email.' is rendered as a standalone paragraph, breaking the list's semantic continuity.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Page 3: '2. In your profile page, click on [icon] next to your email.' and '2. In your profile page, click on [icon] next to your phone number.'
Page 5: '3. Click on [icon] next to the method you want to delete.'
checks.b_evidence_output:
Slide 3 elements index 4 and 5: "content": [{"text": "In your profile page, click on "}], followed by "type": "paragraph", "text": " next to your email.". 
Slide 5 elements index 2, 3, 4: list item ends at "Click on ", followed by image, then paragraph " next to the method you want to delete.".

checks.a_analysis: The URLs provided in the source (specifically 'https://myncid.nc.gov/') are correctly identified and converted into links in the output. No broken, missing, or fabricated links were observed.

checks.a_analysis: While the HTML output converts the multi-column layout of the PDF (e.g., Page 1) into a single-column flow, this is an acceptable adaptation for responsive web design. The sequential order of steps is preserved, allowing the user to find information in a logical progression corresponding to the original document.
The source document does not contain any tables; it is primarily composed of lists and instructional screenshots.

The converted document has severe issues with missing and broken images. Numerous meaningful images and UI components from the source PDF are either missing entirely (leaving blank spaces), replaced with generic green boxes, or mapped incorrectly. This is a significant defect for a user guide that relies on visual identification of buttons and screenshots. For example, in Slide 2, the app icon, QR code instructions, and success message are all broken (green boxes). In Slide 3, edit icons and SEND OTP buttons are missing. In Slide 1, step 4 of the Email section incorrectly displays a 'SUBMIT' button image where the source PDF shows an 'ADD' button image.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains numerous button images (ADD, ADD ENROLLMENT, NEXT, SUBMIT), icons (Edit, Trash), and screenshots (Authenticator App, Scan QR Code screen, Success message) across all 6 pages.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML rendered output, many of these are missing (e.g., Slide 2, steps 4, 5, 7) or rendered as green blocks (e.g., Slide 2 steps 1, 8, 9; Slide 3 VERIFY buttons; Slide 5 REMOVE button; Slide 6 SUBMIT button). Furthermore, incorrect assets are used in Slide 1 (Email step 4 shows 'SUBMIT' instead of 'ADD'; Text Message step 3 shows 'NEXT' instead of 'ADD ENROLLMENT').
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically slides 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6.

checks.a_analysis: There is no visual evidence of duplicated body content. While titles and navigation labels appear in both the sidebar and main body, this is expected for accessible design.
